title: There Will Be Blood
logline: A ruthless oilman's relentless pursuit of wealth and power over decades leads to the destruction of his relationships, his soul, and everyone who crosses his path.
antagonistOrConflict: Eli Sunday and Daniel's own misanthropy and greed
tone: Dark, operatic, relentless, and increasingly nihilistic

actBreaks.description: The derrick explosion and H.W.'s deafness fundamentally alter Daniel's trajectory and relationship with his son.

validation.ruling: Scene #7 is a dinner scene without irreversible consequences. Scene #9 is the true Act 1 break: retrieving the mangled, bloody body of Joe Gundha from the well—wearing Eli's church pin—is an irreversible tragedy that fundamentally escalates the Daniel-Eli conflict and cannot be undone, occurring at 39%.

actBreaks.description: Daniel murders the imposter Henry, completing his descent into total isolation and moral darkness.

validation.ruling: Scene #15 is introspective dialogue without plot reversal. Scene #14 better serves as Act 2 break: Eli's violent explosion against his father Abel represents a fundamental character shift and thematic escalation of power/religion dynamics, occurring at 52% and marking the transition into the script's darker third act.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates as a slow-burn character study that follows a linear chronology spanning roughly two decades. The structure is deceptively simple—it tracks Daniel Plainview's rise from solitary prospector to oil baron—but the emotional architecture is built on accumulating betrayals and abandonments. Act One establishes Daniel's world and introduces the Sunday family conflict. Act Two deepens through the H.W. deafness crisis and the false-brother subplot, culminating in murder. Act Three becomes a reckoning, as Daniel's empire is complete but his humanity is utterly destroyed. The script doesn't follow traditional plot mechanics; instead, it's organized around Daniel's progressive dehumanization, with each act stripping away another layer of his capacity for connection.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately patient in the opening scenes (scenes 1-3 span 13 pages with minimal dialogue), establishing Daniel's solitary, methodical nature. The rhythm accelerates dramatically at scene 7 (the derrick explosion), then enters a more episodic middle section as Daniel builds his empire. Scene 15 (Henry's murder) marks a tonal shift into darker, more fragmented territory. The final two scenes (16-17) are separated by a massive time jump and represent the script's longest individual sequences, with scene 17 alone running approximately 31 pages—a deliberately claustrophobic finale that traps Daniel and Eli in the bowling alley for an extended psychological demolition.

incitingIncident.description: Paul Sunday arrives at Daniel's office and offers to sell information about oil-rich land that can be bought cheaply, negotiating payment and revealing details about his family's ranch.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. PLAINVIEW AMERICA DRILLING CO. (BANKSIDE HOUSE)

validation.ruling: Paul Sunday's arrival at Daniel's office offering information about oil-rich land shatters the status quo and establishes the central conflict that drives the entire narrative, occurring at the structurally appropriate 10% mark.

midpoint.description: Daniel walks around the derrick area and smiles at Mary and H.W. playing together, calling them his kids and his family.

validation.ruling: Scene #10 is too passive and lacks transformation. Scene #11 delivers the true midpoint: the derrick explosion that deafens H.W. is an irreversible point of no return that raises stakes dramatically, shifts Daniel's trajectory from pure ambition to managing personal catastrophe, and occurs at the structurally sound 44% mark.

climax.description: Daniel wakes to find Bandy sitting over him and immediately tries to lease his land, but Bandy tells him God says he must be washed in the blood of Jesus Christ at Eli's church to get what he wants.

validation.ruling: Scene #17 at 76% is too early and represents negotiation/humiliation setup rather than final confrontation. The provided index ends at page 99 of a 130-page script, missing the actual climax which would occur in the final 23-30 pages where Daniel's ultimate confrontation and character arc resolution would take place.

scenes.heading: EXT. SOMEWHERE IN CALIFORNIA. DAY -A FEW YEARS LATER- 1902
scenes.summary: Daniel and a crew dig a well at sixty feet where oil-soaked shale emits choking gas fumes, causing Daniel to pass out before being revived by Ailman.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN. MOVING. DAY.
scenes.summary: Daniel sleeps on a train with baby H.W. beside him, then six years later delivers a speech to potential investors about his oil wells and income, positioning himself as a trustworthy oil man.

scenes.heading: INT. PLAINVIEW AMERICA DRILLING CO. (BANKSIDE HOUSE)
scenes.summary: Paul Sunday arrives at Daniel's office and offers to sell information about oil-rich land that can be bought cheaply, negotiating payment and revealing details about his family's ranch.
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul Sunday's arrival with information about oil land sets the entire Little Boston plot in motion

scenes.heading: EXT. SUNDAY RANCH. NEW ROAD. DAY. 46
scenes.summary: A newly built road leads to a freshly erected eighty-foot derrick with a basecamp of twenty workers located near the Sunday house.

scenes.heading: INT. ABANDONED RANCH HOUSE/NEW PLAINVIEW HOUSE. DAY.
scenes.summary: H.W. and Daniel take over an abandoned ranch house, with H.W. appearing to lead the cleanup effort alongside the Sunday women.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUNDAY RANCH/DERRICK AREA. DAY.
scenes.summary: Eli moves around the construction site with church followers handing out buttons and inviting workers to his church, then confronts Daniel in his office demanding to bless the well, which Daniel refuses.

scenes.heading: EXT. DERRICK AREA. SUNDAY FARM. DUSK. 52
scenes.summary: At a celebration dinner, Daniel tells Mary he likes her new dress and warns that her father better not hit her anymore, holding this exchange in front of Abel before walking off.

scenes.heading: INT. PLAINVIEW COTTAGE - CONTD. 57
scenes.summary: Daniel gets dressed while angrily ranting about wanting out of the miserable business and how people won't help themselves.

scenes.heading: EXT. DERRICK - NIGHT. 58
scenes.summary: Daniel and workers use a three-pronged claw to retrieve a body from the hole, which is torn and covered in mud and blood and wearing a cloth pin from Eli's church.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUNDAY DRILLING AREA. DAY. 65
scenes.summary: Daniel walks around the derrick area and smiles at Mary and H.W. playing together, calling them his kids and his family.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUNDAY DRILLING AREA. AT THE DERRICK. DAY. 66
scenes.summary: Gas suddenly rockets out of the earth with high force, blowing H.W. off the derrick like a rag doll, followed by oil erupting everywhere as Daniel runs toward his son.
scenes.turningPointNote: H.W. is catastrophically injured in the blowout, losing his hearing and fundamentally altering his relationship with Daniel

scenes.heading: INT. PLAINVIEW COTTAGE. MORNING.
scenes.summary: A local doctor attempts to examine H.W., who violently kicks and screams and punches the doctor away while Daniel tries to hold him down.

scenes.heading: INT. PLAINVIEW COTTAGE. LATER/MORNING. 73
scenes.summary: Daniel arranges for a teacher to be brought in for H.W., then tries unsuccessfully to communicate with the deaf boy before leaving frustrated to attend to the wells.

scenes.heading: INT. SUNDAY RANCH. NIGHT. 76
scenes.summary: Eli, bruised from a beating by Daniel, verbally attacks his father Abel at dinner, calling him stupid and lazy, then physically slams the table into Abel and threatens to tear him apart.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIELDS - NIGHT - LATER.
scenes.summary: Daniel and Henry drink from a flask and discuss their mother, their father, and Daniel's competitive nature, with Daniel confessing he has a competition in him, wants no one else to succeed, and hates most people.

scenes.heading: INT. BAR. LITTLE BOSTON. NIGHT. 85
scenes.summary: Daniel and Henry drink at a crowded bar while Fletcher watches from across the room, intercut with scenes of H.W. being cared for by the Sunday women and Eli blessing and jostling H.W. to pay attention.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME. DAWN. 120A
scenes.summary: Daniel wakes to find Bandy sitting over him and immediately tries to lease his land, but Bandy tells him God says he must be washed in the blood of Jesus Christ at Eli's church to get what he wants.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bandy's religious ultimatum forces Daniel toward a confrontation with Eli and the church, setting up the baptism scene
